case.source: Platinum 'Kitchen Remodeling — scope of work' (Kitchen refacing sub-section) + 'Countertop — scope of work' chunk
case.description: Kitchen refacing — homeowner wants to keep the existing cabinet boxes but get new solid wood cabinet doors/drawer fronts, new hardware, and a new quartz countertop. No layout changes, no plumbing relocation.

lines: Confirm permit / plan-check requirement with local building department before starting work (no structural, electrical, or plumbing relocation scope on this job)
Cover and protect all working areas, floors, and adjacent surfaces
Assess existing cabinet boxes: confirm they are square, solid, and free of water damage or delamination before proceeding with refacing
Measure all doors, drawer fronts, and exposed cabinet box faces/end panels
Order new custom solid wood doors and drawer fronts, matching veneer sheet stock for exposed box faces/end panels, and new hardware per customer selection
Remove all existing cabinet doors, drawer fronts, and hardware; label for reference during layout
Clean and lightly sand exposed box faces to ensure veneer adhesion
Apply new veneer skin to exposed cabinet box faces and end panels, trimming flush at edges
Build and install new solid wood doors and drawer fronts, aligning reveals evenly across the run
Install new hinges (soft-close) and drawer slides
Install new hardware (pulls/knobs); drill new holes as needed to match new door design
Install matching trim/molding at crown, light rail, and any exposed panel edges to blend existing boxes with new finish
Touch-up exposed interior box surfaces visible when doors are open
Final adjustment of door/drawer alignment
Site visit to template: measure existing countertop, note sink cutout location and edge profile choice
Disconnect sink and plumbing; shut off water supply at the angle stops
Demo existing countertop and haul away
Inspect and repair cabinet tops/support structure as needed to properly support new quartz weight
Customer to choose quartz slab from sample
Fabrication and cutting of new quartz countertop offsite; template finalized on-site after demo
Install new quartz countertop
Install backsplash (4"/6"/full height per customer selection)
Apply sealer as required
Reinstall sink and faucet; connect all plumbing (supply lines, P-trap, disposal, dishwasher air gap if applicable)
Seal seams and caulk perimeter (backsplash edge, sink edge, wall edge)
Test all connections for leaks
Clean entire job site

notes: Assumed existing sink, faucet, and garbage disposal are being reused and reconnected (not replaced) since client description did not specify new fixtures — confirm with customer.
Assumed quartz per client description; customer to confirm slab selection from sample per standard process.
No electrical, plumbing relocation, or layout changes included per client description, so no rough electrical/plumbing or wall/floor demo scope is listed.
